title: Bridge Essay: Neurocognitive mechanisms of the flow state.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Water/Pleasure** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Water and pleasure — flow state, peak experience, aesthetic pleasure, reward systems, pleasure neuroscience, hedonic tone, musical pleasure.

**Neurocognitive mechanisms of the flow state.** — Prog Brain Res. PMID: 29031465. DOI: 10.1016/bs.pbr.2017.06.012. While the experience of flow is often described in attentional terms-focused concentration or task absorption-specific cognitive mechanisms have received limited interest. We propose that an attentional explanation provides the best way to advance theoretical models and produce practical applications, as well as providing potential solutions to core issues such as how an objectively difficult task can be subjectively effortless.
